draft.introduction: Psalm 51 is David’s penitential prayer after his sin with Bathsheba. It became the supreme model of repentance in both Jewish and Christian liturgy.

translation.text: Have mercy on me, O God, according to your steadfast love; according to your abundant mercy blot out my transgressions. Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin. For I know my transgressions, and my sin is ever before me.
translation.original_text: ἐλέησόν με ὁ θεὸς κατὰ τὸ μέγα ἔλεός σου

translation.text: Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a right spirit within me. Cast me not away from your presence, and take not your Holy Spirit from me. Restore to me the joy of your salvation, and uphold me with a willing spirit.
translation.original_text: καρδίαν καθαρὰν κτίσον ἐν ἐμοί ὁ θεός

translator_notes.note: The verse “in sin did my mother conceive me” became the principal prooftext for Augustine’s doctrine of original sin, though the psalm’s own context is personal confession, not systematic theology.
